description: Add multi-language (i18n) support for Chinese, English, and Japanese

userStories.title: Set up i18n infrastructure with react-i18next
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a lightweight i18n solution set up so all components can use translated strings.
acceptanceCriteria: Install i18next and react-i18next
Create src/i18n/index.ts with i18n initialization
Create src/i18n/locales/zh.json with all Chinese strings
Create src/i18n/locales/en.json with all English strings
Create src/i18n/locales/ja.json with all Japanese strings
Language detection from browser locale (navigator.language), fallback to 'zh'
Wrap AppProvider with I18nextProvider in src/newtab/main.tsx
Export useTranslation hook for components to use
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do NOT use i18next-browser-languagedetector plugin to keep bundle small. Simple navigator.language check is enough for a Chrome extension. The locale JSON files should contain ALL hardcoded Chinese/English text from the entire codebase.

userStories.title: Internationalize types, utils, and constants
userStories.description: As a developer, I need hardcoded strings in types/index.ts, utils/report.ts, utils/date.ts, and utils/storage.ts to use i18n.
acceptanceCriteria: src/types/index.ts: DEFAULT_SETTINGS uses i18n-safe values, ENCOURAGEMENTS array moved to locale files, PRIORITY_CONFIG labels moved to locale files
src/utils/report.ts: all Chinese template strings use t() function or accept translated strings
src/utils/date.ts: date formatting uses locale-aware functions from date-fns (zhCN, enUS, ja locales)
src/utils/storage.ts: Chinese error messages moved to locale files
src/background/index.ts: notification title and message use locale strings
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Background service worker cannot use React hooks. For background.ts, import i18next directly and call t() from the i18n instance. Initialize i18n in background.ts separately with the same config.

userStories.title: Internationalize layout components (Header, BottomBar, NewTab)
userStories.description: As a user, the greeting messages, navigation labels, and loading text should display in my language.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/layout/Header.tsx: all greeting messages moved to locale files, emoji preserved
src/components/layout/BottomBar.tsx: navigation labels from locale files
src/newtab/NewTab.tsx: loading text if any from locale files
All three components use useTranslation hook
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Keep the greeting emoji and time formatting logic in the component, only externalize the text strings.

userStories.title: Internationalize task components (TaskList, TaskItem, TaskForm, TaskFilter)
userStories.description: As a user, all task management UI text should be in my language.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/task/TaskList.tsx: button labels, empty state text, modal titles from locale files
src/components/task/TaskItem.tsx: any UI labels from locale files
src/components/task/TaskForm.tsx: form labels, placeholders, button text, priority labels from locale files
src/components/task/TaskFilter.tsx: filter labels from locale files
All components use useTranslation hook
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Default tags like #工作 #开发 should also be in locale files so they display in the user's language.

userStories.title: Internationalize pomodoro timer
userStories.description: As a user, the pomodoro timer UI text and encouragement messages should be in my language.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/pomodoro/PomodoroTimer.tsx: all UI labels from locale files
Encouragement messages loaded from locale files (zh.json, en.json, ja.json each have their own set)
Status labels from locale files
Task selector placeholder text from locale files
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Japanese encouragement messages should be authentic Japanese phrases, not just translations.

userStories.title: Internationalize work log components (WorkLogList, WorkLogForm, CalendarView)
userStories.description: As a user, work log UI text should be in my language.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/worklog/WorkLogList.tsx: button labels, empty state, date group headers, total hours label from locale files
src/components/worklog/WorkLogForm.tsx: form labels, placeholders, button text from locale files
src/components/worklog/CalendarView.tsx: any UI labels from locale files
All components use useTranslation hook
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Internationalize weekly report component
userStories.description: As a user, the weekly report UI and generated report text should be in my language.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/weekly/WeeklyReport.tsx: all UI labels, stats labels, button text from locale files
Report template default content uses locale-aware strings
Generated report text from locale files
Settings modal labels from locale files
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The weekly report template is user-customizable, so only the DEFAULT template should be locale-aware.

userStories.title: Internationalize settings page and shared UI components
userStories.description: As a user, settings page labels and shared UI component text should be in my language.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/settings/SettingsPage.tsx: all section titles, labels, button text, confirmation dialogs from locale files
src/components/ui/Modal.tsx: close button text from locale
src/components/ui/Toast.tsx: any default text from locale
src/components/ui/Tag.tsx and TagSelector.tsx: any labels from locale
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add language switcher UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a language selector in the settings page so I can switch between Chinese, English, and Japanese.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a language selector in SettingsPage with options: 简体中文, English, 日本語
Selected language is saved to chrome.storage.local and persisted across sessions
Language change takes effect immediately without page reload
date-fns locale also switches accordingly (zhCN/enUS/ja)
Language selector appears at the top of the settings page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser
userStories.notes: Store the language preference as 'yuzutask_language' in chrome.storage.local. On init, check this key first, then fall back to navigator.language.
